paper_title: Multi-Scale Detrended Partial Cross-Correlation Analysis of Tree Ring Width and Climate Variations: Revealing Heat and Drought Stress Resilience Factors in a Forest Ecosystem

methods.description: This method involves the collection and measurement of tree ring widths from various trees to study their growth patterns and assess the quality of cross-chronologies.

steps.description: Trees sampling was carried out according to the methodology adopted in earlier dendrochronological studies.
steps.searchable_summary: collect tree core samples using established dendrochronological methods

steps.description: The cores were extracted from n = 140 trees growing in different parts of the bog and surrounding elevated dry land area with a Pressler borer.
steps.searchable_summary: extract tree cores using a Pressler borer from specified locations

steps.description: Tree ring width (TRW) was measured on a Lintab-6 with the TSAPWin software package.
steps.searchable_summary: measure tree ring width using Lintab-6 and TSAPWin software

steps.description: The quality of the cross-chronologies was assessed using the Cofecha software.
steps.searchable_summary: assess cross-chronology quality using Cofecha software

steps.description: The exact location of each studied tree was determined using a Garmin GPSMAR 62S GPS receiver.
steps.searchable_summary: determine tree locations using Garmin GPSMAR 62S

methods.description: This method is used to quantify long-term persistence in tree-ring data and hydrometeorological variations, focusing on the impact of short-term vs long-term droughts and flash droughts associated with heatwaves.

steps.description: Employ modifications of widely adopted detrended fluctuation analysis (DFA) methods.
steps.searchable_summary: apply modified DFA methods to analyze data

steps.description: Use multi-scale fluctuation analysis to extract relevant information from observational data.
steps.searchable_summary: perform multi-scale fluctuation analysis on observational data

steps.description: Calculate cumulative sums of data series to create profiles.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate cumulative sums to create data profiles

steps.description: Subtract the average from raw data series and calculate profiles for the adjusted data.
steps.searchable_summary: subtract average from data and calculate adjusted profiles
